preFix.title: Repairing a broken sofa seat spring
preFix.subtitle: Access the spring unit through the dust cover, fix or replace a broken coil or sinuous spring, and re-secure the base
preFix.excerpt: How to diagnose and repair a broken seat spring in a sofa. Covers accessing the spring unit through the base dust cover, tying off a broken coil spring or re-clipping a sinuous wire spring, and refitting the cover.

content.text: A broken seat spring announces itself as a single-spot collapse in the seat surface, the padding above the break sinks several centimetres lower than the surrounding cushion. Access is always through the base dust cover, a non-structural black fabric stapled to the underside of the sofa frame. The repair depends on which type of spring is fitted.
Step 1, Open the dust cover and identify the spring type
Turn the sofa on its back. Use the staple remover to lift the dust cover staples along three sides, fold the cover back, and examine the spring unit.
run front-to-back as flat wavy wires.
stand upright in rows and are tied with twine. Identify which spring has broken by pressing the seat surface above each spring from above while you watch from below.
Step 2a, Replacing a sinuous spring clip

Most sinuous spring failures are at the end clip, the metal clip that fastens the spring end to the frame snaps or pulls free. Buy a replacement sinuous spring clip from an upholstery supplier (measure the spring wire diameter to get the right size). Hook the open clip over the spring end, sit the clip on the frame rail, and hammer it flush with the tack hammer. If the spring wire itself is broken, the whole spring must be replaced, unhook both clips, measure the spring length, order a replacement, and clip in from both ends with new clips.
Step 2b, Repairing a broken coil spring tie

If a coil spring has broken free of its twine lashing, the spring will lean and catch on its neighbours. A broken tie can be replaced using 3-ply laid cord: tie a clove hitch onto the top ring of the spring, run the cord across to the nearest adjacent spring, tie another clove hitch, and continue to the frame rail where the cord is stapled and knotted. If the spring itself is broken (a coil has cracked), the entire coil must be replaced, the coil diameter and height must match the others exactly.

Pull the dust cover back into position, fold the raw edge under by 15 mm, and staple through the folded edge at 50 mm intervals using 10 mm T50 staples. The fold prevents the raw edge fraying. Turn the sofa upright and check the seat for level, the repaired section should match the spring height of the adjacent seat units.
